description: Fitz and Simmons move Sarge's body to the lab, amazed that he is healing rapidly despite deadly wounds. The duo tries to rationalize Sarge's uncanny Coulson resemblance and examine the seemingly impossible biology at play. Piper checks in to get any insights about May, while Deke later joins Fitz to contemplate the mysteries around Sarge and the connection to the monoliths.

transcript.line: Thank you. We'll take it from here. Okay.
The resemblance is disturbing. Is it freaking you out, how much he looks like Coulson? ‘Cause it's freaking me out.
Well, that. But also this.
Wait. He has a pulse? That is insane. He was shot four times. He lost practically all of his blood. Why is it taking him so long to die?
No, you don't understand. It's getting stronger. He's coming back.
What is he?
Did she give you an explanation?
Yeah... Sarge, not a fan.
Can't blame her.
I mean, he did kidnap her.
And locked us in with a nuke.
Killed agents in cold blood.
Well, and he has Coulson's friggin' face. I mean, that can't be easy for her.
The world's better off without him.
But why a gun? May doesn't like guns.
She'd beat him to a pulp in a heartbeat.
And she'd enjoy it.
It'd be fun to watch.
But an unarmed man in a cell?
Even if you weren't a fan, you wouldn't...
It's a duck in a barrel. I-It's not her thing. We've all trained with her.
She wouldn't use a gun.
Okay. Piper, go check out May's quarters to see if there's anything out of place. I'll retrace her steps after we got back from Sarge's truck.
And I'll talk to her.
Good. You've been spending the most time with her lately. Maybe she will talk to you.
Give us a minute.
